generated_pair.question: What is the purpose of expressions in MongoDB Query Language (MQL)?
generated_pair.answer: Expressions are MQL components that resolve to a value. They are stateless, meaning they return a value without mutating any of the values used to build the expression.
supporting_sentences: Expressions are MQL components that resolve to a value.
Expressions are stateless, meaning they return a value without mutating any of the values used to build the expression.

generated_pair.question: What is the general form of an expression operator in MongoDB?
generated_pair.answer: Expression operators generally take an array of arguments and have the form: `{ <operator>: [ <argument1>, <argument2> ... ] }`. If an operator accepts a single argument, you can omit the outer array: `{ <operator>: <argument> }`.
supporting_sentences: Expression operators are similar to functions that take arguments. In general, these operators take an array of arguments and have the following form: { <operator>: [ <argument1>, <argument2> ... ] }
If an operator accepts a single argument, you can omit the outer array designating the argument list: { <operator>: <argument> }

generated_pair.question: Which arithmetic operator in MongoDB returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to a specified number?
generated_pair.answer: The `$ceil` operator returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to the specified number.
supporting_sentences: Returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to the specified number.
